description_long: This sculpture depicts a life-sized, athletic male figure standing upright with a contrapposto stance, reminiscent of classical Greco-Roman art. The figure is nude, with well-defined musculature and realistic anatomical proportions, and his left arm is bent while the right arm hangs by his side. The head is slightly turned to the left, and the facial features are finely detailed, with short, curly hair. The figure stands on a rectangular base, and a support structure resembling a tree trunk or drapery is attached to his right leg for stability. The surface of the sculpture appears to be made of light-colored stone, possibly marble, with a matte, slightly rough finish. There are no visible inscriptions, plaques, or additional decorative elements on or near the sculpture.
description: A life-sized, nude male figure in a classical contrapposto pose, sculpted in light-colored stone with realistic anatomical detail and a supporting base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the detailed face, chest, and the left arm bent at the elbow, with the right arm relaxed and the support structure visible by the right leg.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the muscular torso, the bent left arm, and the profile of the face, with the support structure extending behind the right leg.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back musculature, the support structure attached to the right leg, and the overall stance of the figure.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the relaxed right arm, the side of the face, and the support structure more prominently along the right leg.
attribute_location_context: No visible context or environmental setting is provided in the images.
